subject: Science
overall_learning_objectives: Identify the basic steps of nutrition in animals
Recognize the organs of the human digestive system
Understand the simple process of breathing and the organs involved
Identify the components of blood and the role of the heart
Learn about the removal of waste from the body (excretion)
Differentiate between how humans and other animals (like fish or insects) breathe
Appreciate the importance of a healthy lifestyle for body processes

lesson_plan.title: Nutrition: How Animals Eat
topics_covered: Steps of nutrition: Ingestion, Digestion, Absorption, Assimilation, and Egestion
Different ways of taking in food (Sucking, scraping, chewing)
teaching_strategy: Story-telling about the journey of food
Picture cards showing different animals eating
Simple flow diagram on the board
student_activities: Match animals to their mode of feeding
Draw the simple steps of nutrition in their notebooks

lesson_plan.title: The Human Digestive System
topics_covered: Main parts: Mouth, Food pipe, Stomach, Small and Large Intestine
Role of saliva and stomach juices (in easy terms)
teaching_strategy: Using a large human body chart
Demonstration: Crushing a cracker to show mechanical digestion
student_activities: Label a simple diagram of the digestive tract
Act out the path of a piece of bread through the body

lesson_plan.title: Breathing and Respiration
topics_covered: The respiratory system: Nose, Windpipe, and Lungs
Why do we breathe faster when we run?
teaching_strategy: Balloon-and-bottle model to show lung movement
student_activities: Count breaths per minute at rest vs. after jumping
Place hands on chest to feel the ribcage move

lesson_plan.title: Breathing in Other Animals
teaching_strategy: Comparison chart: Lungs vs. Gills vs. Skin
Observation of a live or pictured earthworm
student_activities: Draw a fish and label the gills
Group discussion: 'Can we breathe underwater like a fish?'
assessment: Matching animals to their breathing organs

lesson_plan.title: The Circulatory System: Blood and Heart
topics_covered: What is blood? (Red and White cells made easy)
Blood vessels: Arteries and Veins (Brief intro)
teaching_strategy: Using a 'water pump' analogy for the heart
Finding the pulse in the wrist
student_activities: Feel their own pulse and count the beats
Listen to a friend's heartbeat using a paper cone

lesson_plan.title: Excretion: Cleaning the Body
topics_covered: Sweating as a form of waste removal
student_activities: List 3 things our body removes as waste
assessment: Exit slip: 'Why is sweating good for us?'

teaching_aids: Balloon and plastic bottle lung model
Sieve and sand for filtration demo
teacher_tip: Keep the focus on the 'big picture'—the body is like a machine where every part has a special job to keep us alive. Use plenty of analogies, like the heart being a pump and the digestive system being a kitchen!
